seo.title: Building Code Generation with CrewAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with CrewAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: CrewAI enables multi-agent collaboration for complex tasks like code generation, where agents handle planning, writing, testing, and refining code iteratively. Guardrails are essential to prevent agent delegation loops, hallucinated outputs, task drift, and inconsistent code quality that can lead to unsafe or invalid code. Vex integration ensures reliable, secure code generation by verifying safety, syntax, and execution at each step.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with CrewAI
architectureOverview.description:
In a CrewAI code generation workflow, a Planner Agent decomposes the user request into subtasks and delegates to specialized agents: Coder Agent generates initial code, Tester Agent executes it in a sandbox and generates test cases, Reviewer Agent validates syntax/safety and suggests refinements, and Coordinator Agent orchestrates iterations until success criteria are met. Each agent uses tools for code execution, linting, and dependency management, with outputs passed sequentially through the crew.

The architecture relies on CrewAI's task delegation and memory sharing, but lacks built-in safeguards against infinite loops or malicious code generation. Vex callbacks intercept agent outputs to enforce verification before delegation proceeds.

This setup supports iterative refinement: failed tests trigger re-delegation to Coder/Reviewer, with human-in-loop approval for high-risk changes.
architectureOverview.diagram:
User Request
    |
[Planner Agent] --> Task Decomposition
    |
+--[Coder Agent] --> Generate Code --> [Vex Verify: Syntax/Safety]
|   |
|   +--[Sandbox Executor] --> Run Tests
|
+--[Tester Agent] --> Test Results --> [Vex Verify: Execution]
    |
[Reviewer Agent] --> Refinements --> [Coordinator] --> Iterate/Complete

risks.name: Agent delegation loops
risks.description: Planner endlessly re-delegates between Coder and Tester due to vague success criteria
risks.mitigation: Vex task callback enforces max iterations and delegation graph validation

risks.name: Hallucinated tool outputs
risks.description: Agents fabricate sandbox execution results or fake test passes
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies tool outputs against actual execution traces and schemas

risks.name: Malicious code generation
risks.description: Coder agent generates credential-stealing or sandbox-escape code
risks.mitigation: Vex scans for safety violations, dependency risks, and credential patterns

risks.name: Task drift in refinement
risks.description: Reviewer reinterprets objectives, generating unrelated code during iterations
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces objective alignment and semantic similarity checks

implementation.title: Basic CrewAI Code Generation Crew
implementation.description: Set up core agents and tasks for code generation workflow without guardrails
implementation.code:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o")

coder = Agent(
    role='Senior Python Developer',
    goal='Write clean, efficient Python code that passes all tests',
    backstory='10+ years experience in production codebases',
    llm=llm,
    verbose=True
)

tester = Agent(
    role='QA Engineer',
    goal='Validate code functionality and generate comprehensive tests',
    llm=llm,
    verbose=True
)

task1 = Task(
    description='Write a Python function that calculates fibonacci numbers efficiently',
    agent=coder
)

task2 = Task(
    description='Test the fibonacci function with edge cases and performance benchmarks',
    agent=tester,
    context=[task1]
)

crew = Crew(agents=[coder, tester], tasks=[task1, task2])
result = crew.kickoff()

implementation.title: Sandbox Code Execution Tool
implementation.description: Custom tool for safe code execution with timeout and dependency controls
implementation.code:
from crewai_tools import BaseTool
from subprocess import run, PIPE
import tempfile
import os

class SandboxExecutor(BaseTool):
    name: str = "sandbox_executor"
    description: str = "Executes Python code in isolated sandbox with timeout"
    
    def _run(self, code: str, timeout: int = 30) -> str:
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode='w', suffix='.py', delete=False) as f:
            f.write(code)
            temp_file = f.name
        try:
            result = run(['python', temp_file], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=timeout)
            os.unlink(temp_file)
            return f"STDOUT: {result.stdout}\nSTDERR: {result.stderr}\nEXIT: {result.returncode}"
        except Exception as e:
            os.unlink(temp_file)
            return f"ERROR: {str(e)}"

# Usage in agent
tester_tools = [SandboxExecutor()]
tester = Agent(..., tools=tester_tools)

implementation.title: Iterative Refinement Coordinator
implementation.description: Coordinator agent manages refinement loops with convergence criteria
implementation.code:
coordinator = Agent(
    role='Project Coordinator',
    goal='Ensure code meets all requirements through iterative refinement',
    backstory='Expert in agile development and code review processes',
    llm=llm,
    verbose=True
)

refine_task = Task(
    description="""Review test results from {test_results}. If failing:
    1. Identify specific failures
    2. Delegate fixes to appropriate agents
    3. Track iteration count (max 5)
    If passing: approve final code.""",
    agent=coordinator,
    context=[task1, task2],
    max_iterations=5
)

crew = Crew(agents=[coder, tester, coordinator], tasks=[task1, task2, refine_task])

implementation.title: Code Safety Pre-Commit Hook
implementation.description: Basic linting and safety checks before agent delegation

vexIntegration.title: Vex Task Callback for Agent Outputs
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex verification as callback on every task completion to catch failures early
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient
from crewai import Task

vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

def vex_task_callback(task, result):
    # Verify code safety and syntax
    verification = vex.verify({
        "input": task.description,
        "output": result.raw,
        "policy": "code-generation-high-risk",
        "checks": ["code_safety", "syntax_validity", "no_credential_leakage"]
    })
    
    if not verification.passed:
        raise ValueError(f"Vex blocked unsafe output: {verification.issues}")
    return result

# Apply to all tasks
task1.callback = vex_task_callback
task2.callback = vex_task_callback
refine_task.callback = vex_task_callback

vexIntegration.description: Wrap sandbox execution with Vex dependency and execution safety checks

vexIntegration.description: Prevent delegation loops with Vex state tracking across crew execution
